summary: Iteration 11 re-verifies the Bearer-token fallback fix flagged in iter 10. Frontend wiring is now CORRECT — authLogin, authRegister, and authLogout all touch setBearerToken as promised in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js. All five review-request cases PASS end-to-end against the live preview URL: (1) primary bug repro — login as admin, clear BOTH access_token+refresh_token cookies, click Generate on D2740 with 'Fractured cusp' + tooth 14: /api/generate returns 200 with `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` header, narrative renders, URL stays on '/'; (2) authenticated GET /api/history after cookie clear returns 200 with same Bearer header; (3) new-user register also seeds the in-memory bearer — /api/generate works after clearing cookies post-register (200); (4) logout clears the bearer — post-logout /api/auth/me (no cookies, no bearer) returns 401 as expected; (5) cookies-intact regression: login + generate returns 200. Backend suite backend_iter10_test.py still 9/9 green.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is minimal and correct: authLogin/authRegister call setBearerToken(data.access_token) after awaiting the response; authLogout wraps in try/finally so the bearer is nuked even if the network call fails. Request interceptor at lines 20-25 attaches Authorization only when a bearer is present, and the response interceptor at lines 66-68 keeps the bearer refreshed on 401->refresh cycles. Belt-and-suspenders logic is solid.

Bearer token lives in module scope (not localStorage) — good XSS hygiene, but a hard refresh will drop it and rely purely on the httpOnly refresh cookie. That's the intended tradeoff and is documented in the comment at lines 6-8.

Nit (non-blocking): during login the app fires three /api/auth/me calls that 401 before the login submit — expected pre-auth probes from AuthContext, but consider gating them on a session hint to keep console clean.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5 review-request scenarios: primary repro, bearer history GET, register bearer, logout clears bearer, cookies-intact regression)

test_credentials: admin@dental.com / admin123 (seeded); register-flow test creates TEST_iter11_<uuid>@dental.com per run
seed_data_creation: Register flow creates one TEST_iter11_<hex>@dental.com user in this run. Not cleaned up — safe to purge from users collection on next Mongo reset.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 'Generate logs me out' bug is FIXED end-to-end. Frontend Bearer wiring in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js is verified. Backend Bearer fallback in server.py + auth.py was already verified in iter10 (do not retest). Regression coverage: /app/backend/tests/backend_iter10_test.py + backend_iter8_test.py.
rca of the issue: Iter 10 root cause was: authLogin/authRegister never called setBearerToken, so the in-memory bearer stayed null and the request interceptor had nothing to attach when cookies were dropped. Iter 11 fix (verified): authLogin/authRegister now `const data = await api.post(...).then(r => r.data); if (data?.access_token) setBearerToken(data.access_token); return data;` and authLogout wraps the network call in try/finally with setBearerToken(null). Verified live: cookies-cleared /api/generate now succeeds (200) with Authorization: Bearer header set by the axios request interceptor, and user is NOT redirected to /login.
